title: Commentary on Policy/Limits Compliance: December 31, 2026
content:
Campus remains facing notable compliance gaps while maintaining strong asset quality and capital positions; however, efficiency ratios exceed limits alongside profitability shortfalls in return metrics. 

Asset Quality
All classified assets to net worth, delinquent loans to total loans, and charge-offs remain within policy thresholds with no material exceptions identified.

Capital & Solvency
Net worth ratio of 10% meets the minimum target while solvency evaluation at 110% exceeds benchmarks. Net worth growth achieved its annualized objective without deviation from approved targets.

Profitability & Growth
Operating expenses to average assets breach limits, and return on average assets falls below established thresholds; however, loan growth remains within range despite membership expansion lagging behind expectations.

Interest Rate & Funding
Cost of funds aligns with acceptable ranges while net interest margin meets minimum yield requirements. Yield on loans sustains adequate levels relative to funding costs.

Liquidity & Mix
Cash and short-term investments stay within prescribed limits, though regular shares as a percentage of total liabilities fall below target benchmarks. Loan concentration metrics remain appropriately balanced across asset classes.

Closing: Campus maintains robust capital buffers while addressing elevated expense ratios that constrain profitability margins alongside insufficient membership growth rates requiring strategic intervention.
